operating_state: Net revenues increased 16% to $53.51 billion driven by significantly higher other principal transactions revenues from private equity gains, 24% higher investment banking revenues from leveraged finance and equity underwriting, and 27% higher net interest income. Global Banking & Markets revenues rose with strong FICC and equities intermediation performance, while Asset & Wealth Management revenues increased 16% from higher management fees and increased assets under supervision. The firm completed the GreenSky sale and transitioned the GM credit card program while retaining Apple Card operations.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased 2% to $33.77 billion despite higher compensation, as significant prior-year charges did not recur including commercial real estate impairments and consumer platform write-downs. Efficiency ratio improved to 63.1% from 74.6% as revenue growth outpaced expense increases. The firm maintains substantial technology investments in electronic trading platforms, AI integration, cybersecurity infrastructure, and regulatory compliance systems.
material_events: Returned $11.80 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. The Federal Reserve increased Goldman's stress capital buffer from 5.5% to 6.2%, raising the CET1 capital ratio requirement to 13.7% through September 2025. Formed Capital Solutions Group in Global Banking & Markets in January 2025 to provide comprehensive financing services. Completed historical principal investments dispositions during 2024, targeting majority selldown by end of 2026.
macro_exposures: Escalating U.S.-China tensions and proposed tariff increases create cross-border transaction risks, while U.S. debt ceiling suspension expires in 2025 presenting Treasury market volatility. Geopolitical tensions from Ukraine conflict and Middle East tensions remain elevated alongside inflation and central bank policy uncertainty. G-SIB surcharge increases from 3.0% to 3.5% beginning in 2026, with Basel III implementation timelines varying by jurisdiction alongside climate-related physical and transition risks.
forward_outlook: Management targets through-the-cycle ROE of 14-16% and ROTE of 15-17% with 2024 achieving 12.7% and 13.5% respectively. Asset & Wealth Management targeting ROE in mid-teens within medium term and growing total credit alternative assets by end of 2028. Platform Solutions expects pre-tax breakeven by end of 2025. The firm anticipates continued material investments in AI technologies and electronic trading platforms while pursuing selective acquisitions.
uncertainty_profile: Credit rating downgrades could trigger collateral posting requirements with escalating amounts for multiple-notch downgrades. Resolution strategy implementation could result in complete losses for Group Inc. security holders while subsidiary creditors receive full recoveries. Provision for credit losses totaled $1.35 billion with Platform Solutions contributing primarily from credit card charge-offs. Unresolved contingencies include liability for unrecognized tax benefits and ongoing litigation reserves.
